messages.author: Max
messages.text: Initial testing looks good, I like it. Found a few bugs - Updating the radio parameters & config via https://meshkit.nl/api/radio/config gives a 500, although it does seem to properly make the change? However, after doing that, if I refresh the page and try to re-connect the radio, the socket times out and the site will not work, seemingly until I manually restart the companion? Not sure why I have to restart the device though, since its definitely an issue with the socket, bc i can still use the official USB terminal or my own instance of remoteterm

However, after doing that, if I refresh the page and try to re-connect the radio, the socket times out and the site will not work, seemingly until I manually restart the companion? Not sure why I have to restart the device though, since its definitely an issue with the socket, bc i can still use the official USB terminal or my own instance of remoteterm

messages.author: Killer4nl
messages.text: I will add credits for open source repo’s i used
messages.content_html: I will add credits for open source repo’s i used
messages.reply_html:
FlightlessApteryx | AE9Y
And @Killer4nl gotta be honest having a "MeshKit is open source software" and a "Created by Killer4nl" is pretty uncool given that there is not a link to the source, and that this is a frontend-serial rebrand of my codebase, but ultimately, the MIT license permits uncool behavior. But it doesn't permit usage without republishing the license with the original copyright headers intact.

messages.author: FlightlessApteryx | AE9Y
messages.text: Thank you. Building things is awesome, but there are licensing frameworks that you are legally compelled to do that under; strongly recommend carefully reading LICENSE.md for any project you extend. For projects building RemoteTerm, that's under MIT license, which means the clear inclusion of the copyright banner and full license text "in all copies or substantial portions of the Software" ( https://github.com/jkingsman/Remote-Terminal-for-MeshCore/blob/main/LICENSE.md) . I think it's also just part of being a decent person but ultimately that is a subjective judgement; all you are strictly compelled to do is license + copyright inclusion.

messages.author: FlightlessApteryx | AE9Y
messages.text: I'm curious, is this shifting all packet storage to the frontend, or is the local bluetooth just proxied to the backend? If it's the latter, am I correct that you're storing DMs and chats server-side? Is there any key storage server-side?
messages.content_html: I'm curious, is this shifting all packet storage to the frontend, or is the local bluetooth just proxied to the backend? If it's the latter, am I correct that you're storing DMs and chats server-side? Is there any key storage server-side?

messages.author: Killer4nl
messages.text: In my current implementation the backend is acting as the WebSocket bridge to the local radio/companion, and DMs/chats are stored server-side (mainly for persistence across sessions/devices). Encryption keys are NOT stored server-side, those are read from the device / companion side, and the server does not keep any key material. Also: I want to do this properly, so I’m going to temporarily take the tool offline until I’ve fixed everythinngg Thanks a lot for the help and for pointing this out , I genuinely appreciate it
messages.content_html: In my current implementation the backend is acting as the WebSocket bridge to the local radio/companion, and DMs/chats are stored server-side (mainly for persistence across sessions/devices). Encryption keys are NOT stored server-side, those are read from the device / companion side, and the server does not keep any key material. Also: I want to do this properly, so I’m going to temporarily take the tool offline until I’ve fixed everythinngg Thanks a lot for the help and for pointing this out , I genuinely appreciate it
